translations.source_text: 夏，齊孝公卒，有齊怨，不廢喪紀，禮也
translations.translation:
Liu Yao (Zhengli)
劉繇 (正禮)
Lived AD 157–198
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 49 (Wu 4)
byZL181
onMarch 24th, 2025

Liu Yao, styledZhengli, was fromMouping[牟平],Donglai[東萊].
The young son of theFilialPrinceofQi[齊孝王]1was enfeoffed as theMarquisofMouping[牟平侯], and his descendants would reside there. The elder uncle of[Liu] Yaowas[Liu] Chong, who served as theGrand CommandantunderHan.AThe older brother of[Liu] Yaowas[Liu] Dai, styledGongshan, who would eventually becomePalace AttendantandInspectorofYan province[兗州刺史].B•••
TheFilialPrinceofQi[齊孝王] here is Liu Jianglü [劉將閭], grandson ofEmperor Liu Bangthrough his son Liu Fei [劉肥].↩
TheBook of Continued Hanstates: The grandfather of[Liu] Yaowas [Liu] Ben [劉本], who received teachings on the classics and commentaries, and had extensive knowledge of various books, being regarded as a comprehending Ruist. He was nominated asWise and GoodandFoursquare and Forthright, then served asChiefofBan[般長]. He died in office.
[Liu] Chongwas styledZurong. He received the undertaking of his father. As he was knowledgeable in the classics and conducted himself well, he was nominated asFilial and Incorrupt. TheMinister of the Householdrecommended him as having the Four Qualities2and he was promoted toPrefectofDongpingling[東平陵令]. He took on duties for several years, then left his office when his mother fell ill. The common people clung to the carriage and pushed back the wheels, congesting the roads. The vehicle was unable to advance, so he parked, wore plain clothes, and snuck out. He would return to nurture [his mother].
Later, he was recruited into the bureau of theGrand Generaland was later promoted toGrand AdministratorofKuaiji[會稽太守]. He conducted himself with uprightness, being a model for his subordinates. There was great governance in his commandery. He was summoned to serve asCourt Architect. The common people ofShanyin county[山陰縣] were tens of miles away from the commandery seat, they being located in the hills and valleys of Ruxie [若邪]. There were five or six aged elders who were all seventy to eighty years old, and when they heard that[Liu] Chongwas relocating, they brought each other to see[Liu] Chongoff together. One man offered one hundred cash coins.[Liu] Chongsaw them and invited them, stating, “Why must you elders toil to travel such long distances?” They all replied, “We rustic elders of the hills and valleys have never once in our lives went to the commandery or counties. In previous times, the officials would demand [resources] without leaving, and there were nights among the common people where the barking of dogs would not cease, resulting in the common people being without rest in the morning. Ever since you,wise administrator, have dismounted your chariot [to serve here], the dogs have no longer barked at night and the officials are rarely among the common people anymore. We aged elders are very fortunate to witness such sagacious governance. Now that we heard that you are leaving us, we mustered all our strength to come here to see you off.”[Liu] Chongthanked them and selected one large cash coin, henceKuaiji[會稽] would nickname[Liu] Chongas “TheGrand Administratorwho Takes One Coin”. His incorruptness was of this sort.
[Liu] Chongserved in two commanderies, occupied the nine [ministerial] positions eight times, and ascended to the three duties [of excellencies] four times. He had not reserved his wealth in his household and he did not have any accumulated treasures or goods. He would often have humble meals, thin clothes, poor vehicles, and weak horses. Hence, he was regarded as being lacking. After his third removal from paramount positions, he returned to his homeland. Whenever he left or entered the capital, he often abstained from using horses on the roads such that no one knew who he was.[Liu] Chongonce wished to stay at a pavilion, but the pavilion official stayed him, saying, “We tidied this lodging residence to awaitExcellency Liu. You may not stay here.”[Liu] Chongthen passed through and left. His purity and frugality were all of this sort. He died of illness in his old age at hishousehold.•••
TheMinister of the Householdwould nominate officials for higher rank based on four qualities: sincerity, austerity, deference, and morality.↩
TheBook of Continued Hanstates: The father of[Liu] Yaowas [Liu] Yu [劉輿], another name being [Liu] Fang [劉方], who was theGrand AdministratorofShanyang[山陽太守].[Liu] Daiand[Liu] Yaowere both outstanding talents.
TheRecords of Famed Heroesacclaims[Liu] Daias being filial, fraternal, benevolent, and forgiving, humbling himself in receiving others.
When[Liu] Yaowas nineteen years old, the cousin of his father, [Liu] Wei [劉韙], was taken hostage by bandits.[Liu] Yaosought and received him, then returned. For this, he was held in esteem.
He was nominated asFilial and Incorrupt, served as aPalace Gentleman, and madeChiefofXiayi[下邑長]. At the time, the commandery administrator made requests of him because he was of a noble clan, so he abandoned his post and left.
The province recruited him to serve inJi’nan[濟南]. TheChancellorofJi’nan[濟南相] was the son of aRegular Palace Attendant, and he was greedy, corrupt, and disobedient.[Liu] Yaopetitioned to impeach him.
Taoqiu HongofPingyuan[平原] recommended[Liu] Yaoand wanted him to be nominated asOutstanding Talent. TheGrand Administratorsaid, “Last year, we nominatedGongshan. Why should we nominateZhenglias well?”[Taoqiu] Hongreplied, “If Your Enlightened Lordship would useGongshanat the front and haveZhengliat the rear, this is akin to the sayings of ‘steering two dragons over a long distance’ and ‘urging legendary steeds over a thousand miles’. Why not do this?” He was to be recruited to serve as abureaucratunder theExcellency of Worksand appointed him to serve asImperial Clerk, but he did not go.
He escaped the chaos toHuaipu[淮浦]. An imperial edict appointed him to serve asInspectorofYang province[揚州刺史]. At the time,Yuan Shuwas inHuainan[淮南].[Liu] Yaowas afraid of him, so he did not dare go to the province. He wanted to ferry south across theYangzi River[江].Wu JingandSun Benwelcomed him toQu’e[曲阿].[Yuan] Shuplotted with usurpative ambitions, so he attacked and captured many of the commanderies and counties.[Liu] YaosentFan NengandZhang Yingto garrison along the banks of theYangzi River[江] to repel them. Because[Wu] Jingand[Sun] Benwere appointed by[Yuan] Shu, he forced them out. Thus, when[Yuan] Shuproclaimed himself asInspectorofYang province[揚州刺史], he,[Wu] Jing, and[Sun] Benjoined forces to attack[Zhang] Yingand[Fan] Neng, who did not fall for several years.[Liu] Yaowas ordered byHanto serve asGovernorandGeneral who Inspires Might. His host numbered in the tens of thousands.
When[Sun] Ceferried east, he defeated the likes of[Zhang] Yingand[Fan] Neng.[Liu] Yaofled toDantu[丹徒],Cgoing upstream in theJiangnan region[江南] to protectYuzhangand to station atPengze[彭澤].Ze Rongarrived before him,Dkilled theGrand Administrator,Zhu Hao,Eand entered to occupy the commandery.[Liu] Yaoadvanced to suppress[Ze] Rong, but was defeated by[Ze] Rong. He recruited and united the counties subordinates to him, then attacked and defeated[Ze] Rong.[Ze] Rongwas routed and fled into the mountains, later killed by the common people.[Liu] Yaosoon fell ill and died. He was forty-two years old at the time.
TheAnnals of HanbyYuan Hongstates:Liu Yaowas to flee toKuaiji[會稽], butXuZijiangsaid, "Kuaiji[會稽], being abundant and wealthy, is coveted by[Sun] Ce; moreover, you would be trapped in a coastal region. You should not stay there.Yuzhang[豫章] is better, for north to it is the land ofYu[豫] and west to it is the province ofJing[荊州]. If you assemble the officials and commoners together, then send messengers to present tribute [to the court], you will be known byCao ofYan province. Even ifYuanGongluis obstructing the area in between, he will not last long, for he is as a dhole and a wolf. Once you have received royal commands,MengdeandJingshengwill be sure to aid you.”[Liu] Yaoheeded him.
Ze [笮] has the fanqie of Zhuang [壯] and Li [力].
TheChronicles of the Astute Emperorstates: That year,[Liu] Yaogarrisoned atPengze[彭澤]. He sent[Ze] Rongto assistZhu Haoin suppressingZhuge Xuan, theGrand Administratorappointed byLiu Biao.XuZijiangtold[Liu] Yao, “Ze Ronghas marched out with the army, but he is someone who disregards having a upright reputation.ZhuWenmingis fond of promoting sincerity and he is trusting in people. You should send someone to secretly warn him.” When[Ze] Rongcame, he indeed deceived and killed[Zhu] Hao, replacing him in managing the affairs of the commandery.
***
Ze Rongwas fromDanyang[丹楊]. He previously gathered a force of hundreds, then went to rely on theGovernorofXu province[徐州牧],Tao Qian.[Tao] Qiansent him to supervise the transports of grain in the waterways ofGuangling[廣陵] andPengcheng[彭城]. He, being unchecked, killed indiscriminately, then occupied three commanderies and had the delivery of goods go to him. Thus, he engaged in the largescale construction of a temple for the Buddha. He used copper to make the figures and used gold to paint the bodies, clothing them in vibrant brocades. He suspended nine sets of bronze plates above, while below, he made a storied pavilion with walkways that could hold over three thousand people, and he had all of them recite Buddhist classics. He told people in his land or in bordering commanderies who were fond of Buddhism to listen and receive its philosophy, beckoning them by exempting them from any other labor. Thus, the amount of those who came from afar or near numbered over five thousand persons and families. Each time the statue of The Buddha was washed, many meals of wine and cooked rice were set up and mats were laid out across the road, traversing tens of miles. The people who came to watch and eat numbered ten thousand. The costs were millions upon millions.
WhenExcellency CaoattackedTao Qian, the people ofXu[徐] were disturbed and displaced.[Ze] Rongled ten thousand men and women and three thousand horses to flee toGuangling[廣陵]. TheGrand AdministratorofGuangling[廣陵太守],Zhao Yu, treated him with etiquette befitting a guest. Previously, theChancellorofPengcheng[彭城相],Xue Li, was forced out byTao Qian, so he garrisoned atMoling[秣陵].[Ze] Rongcoveted the armies ofGuangling[廣陵], so he intoxicated and killed[Zhao] Yu, then allowed his soldiers to plunder greatly, leaving after loading [the loot]. He killed[Xue] Li, then killed[Zhu] Hao.
***
Later,[Sun] Cewent west to conquerJiangxia[江夏], and when he returned throughYuzhang[豫章], he collected the remains of[Liu] Yaoand treated his family quite well.Wang Langsent[Sun] Cea message, stating:
“In the past, whenLiuZhenglifirst governed the province, he was unable to reach it himself, so he verily relied on your esteemed family to do. Hence, he was able to cross theYangzi River[江] to be able to serve and have a place that was secured. The courtesy of him entering his border and the feelings of gratitude and camaraderie have always existed from beginning to end. Later, due to his enmity with theYuan clan, disharmony slowly began to fester, and you went from being his comrade to being his foe. In his mind, he felt really unhappy. After some peace, he often expressed wishes that amends could be made and become old friends once more. Alas, once you two were separated, his sincere intentions could no longer be expressed, and then he perished suddenly! This is quite sorrowful! I know that using sincerity to discipline the weak, using kindness to repay the spiteful, collecting the corpse, raising the orphans, lamenting the dead, showing compassion towards the living, abandoning past suspicions, and protecting entrusted children is indubitably the deep expression of grace and kindness and the quintessential meaning of a brilliant reputation! In antiquity, the people of Lu [魯], despite having enmity with Qi [齊], did not abandon the period of mourning, which theChroniclesregarded as virtuous and professed to be the fulfillment of etiquette.3Such an act was truly deserving of being recorded in refined histories and being praised in township schools. Theeldest sonofZhenglihas aspirations and moral qualities. I think that he must be extraordinary. You have so much power to carry out punishment, but would it also not be great to extendkindness?”•••
The states of Qi and Lu were enemies with each other for many generations of rulers. When theFilialDukeof Qi [齊孝公] died, the state of Lu still expressed condolences for his death. The commentaries to theChroniclesstated of this, “In the summer, theFilialDukeof Qi died. There was enmity with Qi, but we did not abandon the period of mourning. Such is the proper etiquette.” [夏，齊孝公卒，有齊怨，不廢喪紀，禮也].↩
***
The oldest son of[Liu] Yaowas[Liu] Ji, styledJingyu. When he was fourteen years old, he observed the mourning period of[Liu] Yaowith the utmost etiquette. The former officials [ofLiu Yao] offered him gifts, but he refused them all.FHe was handsome and pleasing in his features.Sun Quanespecially cherished and respected him, and when[Sun] Quanserved asGeneral of Coursers, he recruited him to serve asbureaucrat of the Eastern Bureauand conferred him to beColonel who Assists RighteousnessandHousehold General who Establishes Loyalty.
TheBook of Wustates:[Liu] Jiencountered many hardships and suffered from misery and discomfort. He lived in seclusion and studied ways of thinking, so he never felt despair. He and his younger brothers resided together. He often slept late and got up early, and his wife and concubines rarely saw his face. His younger brothers respected and feared him, and treated him like their father. He was not careless in making friends such that within his gates, there were no random guests.
When[Sun] Quanbecame King ofWu, he promoted[Liu] JitoMinister of Finance. Once,[Sun] Quanfeasted and drank whenCavalry CommandantYu Fangot intoxicated and acted perversely.[Sun] Quanwanted to kill him, his fury very extreme, but[Liu] Jimade stringent protests. Thus,[Yu] Fanwas able to avoid such.[Sun] Quan, during the Great Heat solar term, once feasted and drank in a boat when there was a thunderstorm above the boat.[Sun] Quanused a canopy to cover himself, and he also ordered for it to cover[Liu] Ji; the other people did not receive such. His [Liu Ji] close treatment were all of these types.
He was promoted toDirector of the Palace Gentlemen. After[Sun] Quanclaimed the imperial title, he was shifted to serve asMinister of the Householdand shared the role ofCommentator on Affairs of the Secretariat. When he was forty-nine, he died. Later,[Sun] Quanhad his son,[Sun] Ba, marry the daughter of[Liu] Jiand bestowed them a mansion and its area. They were given lavish gifts in each of the four seasons such that they were comparable to the Quan and Zhang families.4The two younger brothers of[Liu] Ji,[Liu] Shuoand[Liu] Shang, both served asCavalry Commandant.•••
Quan CongandZhang Chenghad marriages to the royal Sun clan.↩
